value: WebGL game optimization without WebGPU. Top wins: (1) ANGLE_instanced_arrays / WebGL2 instanced drawing -- 400 objects -> 1 draw call, ~99% CPU overhead drop. (2) OES_vertex_array_object VAO -- replaces N attrib calls with 1 bind. (3) Compressed textures via WEBGL_compressed_texture_s3tc/etc/astc -- stay compressed on GPU. (4) CPU frustum culling -- cut GPU work, not just clipping. (5) gl state cache JS side -- skip redundant useProgram/bindTexture calls. (6) Draw call sorting: opaque front-to-back, transparent back-to-front, sorted by shader then texture. (7) mediump precision in fragment shaders -- mobile GPU bandwidth. (8) bufferSubData not bufferData for per-frame data. (9) Object pooling / typed arrays to eliminate GC. (10) OffscreenCanvas + Worker to free main thread. Critical: avoid discard (kills early-Z), avoid dynamic branching in frag shader, push vertex-count work to vertex shader. WebGL 2 adds built-in instancing, UNSIGNED_INT indices, VAO, no extension negotiation needed.
